sentences.target: Ich fange an, Montage zu hassen.

translations.de: Ich fange an, Montage zu hassen.
translations.es: Estoy empezando a odiar los lunes.
translations.fr: Je commence à détester les lundis.
translations.it: Sto iniziando a odiare il lunedì.
translations.pt: Estou começando a odiar as segundas-feiras.
translations.he: אני מתחיל לשנוא את ימי שני.
translations.vi: Tôi bắt đầu ghét ngày thứ Hai.
translations.nl: Ik begin een hekel aan maandagen te krijgen.
translations.hi: मुझे सोमवार से नफ़रत होने लगी है.
translations.ro: Încep să urăsc zilele de luni.
translations.da: Jeg er begyndt at hade mandage.
translations.ja: 月曜日が嫌いになってきた。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun in nominative serving as subject of the finite verb.
Finite verb (1st person singular, present) of the separable verb 'anfangen', heading the clause and selecting a zu-infinitive complement.
Separable verb particle that combines with 'fange' to form 'anfangen' (to start/begin).
Plural common noun functioning as the direct object inside the infinitival clause ('zu hassen').
Infinitival particle marking the non-finite verb 'hassen' in the complement clause.
Infinitive verb within the zu-infinitive complement selected by 'anfangen'; takes 'Montage' as its object.

sentences.target: Sie scheint Kaffee plötzlich zu hassen.

translations.de: Sie scheint Kaffee plötzlich zu hassen.
translations.en: She seems to suddenly hate coffee.
translations.es: Parece que de repente odia el café.
translations.fr: Elle semble soudain détester le café.
translations.it: Sembra che all'improvviso odi il caffè.
translations.ko: 그녀가 갑자기 커피를 싫어하게 된 것 같아.
translations.zh-Hans: 她好像突然不喜欢咖啡了。
translations.yue: 佢好似突然間憎咗咖啡。
translations.pt: Parece que ela de repente passou a odiar café.
translations.tr: Sanki birden kahveden nefret etmeye başladı.
translations.vi: Có vẻ như cô ấy bỗng dưng ghét cà phê.
translations.ar: يبدو أنها بدأت فجأة تكره القهوة.
translations.nl: Het lijkt alsof ze ineens een hekel aan koffie heeft gekregen.
translations.hu: Úgy tűnik, hirtelen megutálta a kávét.
translations.hi: लगता है कि उसे अचानक कॉफ़ी से नफ़रत हो गई है.
translations.pl: Wydaje się, że nagle znienawidziła kawę.
translations.el: Φαίνεται πως ξαφνικά μίσησε τον καφέ.
translations.nb: Det virker som om hun plutselig begynte å hate kaffe.
translations.id: Kelihatannya dia tiba-tiba jadi benci kopi.
translations.sv: Det verkar som att hon plötsligt började hata kaffe.
translations.ro: Se pare că a început dintr-odată să urască cafeaua.
translations.cs: Zdá se, že najednou začala nenávidět kávy.
translations.da: Det lader til, at hun pludselig er begyndt at hade kaffe.
translations.fi: Vaikuttaa siltä, että hän yhtäkkiä alkoi vihata kahvia.
translations.ja: 彼女は急にコーヒーが嫌いになったみたい。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun in nominative serving as subject of the finite verb.
Finite verb (3rd person singular, present) functioning as a raising verb, selecting a zu-infinitive complement.
Singular common/mass noun serving as the direct object of 'hassen' within the infinitival clause.
Adverb modifying the infinitive 'hassen', indicating manner/temporal nuance.
Infinitival particle marking the non-finite verb 'hassen' in the complement clause.
Infinitive verb in the zu-infinitive complement of 'scheint'; takes 'Kaffee' as its object, modified by 'plötzlich'.

sentences.target: Wir versuchen, Mathe nicht zu hassen.

translations.de: Wir versuchen, Mathe nicht zu hassen.
translations.en: We're trying not to hate math.
translations.es: Estamos intentando no odiar las matemáticas.
translations.fr: On essaie de ne pas détester les maths.
translations.it: Stiamo cercando di non odiare la matematica.
translations.zh-Hans: 我们正努力不去讨厌数学。
translations.vi: Chúng tôi đang cố gắng không ghét môn toán.
translations.nl: We proberen geen hekel aan wiskunde te hebben.
translations.hi: हम कोशिश कर रहे हैं कि हमें गणित से नफ़रत न हो.
translations.el: Προσπαθούμε να μην μισούμε τα μαθηματικά.
translations.nb: Vi prøver å ikke hate matematikk.
translations.id: Kami berusaha untuk tidak membenci matematika.
translations.sv: Vi försöker att inte hata matte.
translations.da: Vi prøver at lade være med at hade matematik.
translations.ja: 数学を嫌いにならないように頑張ってる。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun in nominative serving as subject of the finite verb.
Finite verb (1st person plural, present) selecting a zu-infinitive complement.
Singular common noun functioning as the direct object within the infinitival clause headed by 'hassen'.
Negation particle scoping over the infinitival VP ('zu hassen'), negating the action.
Infinitival particle marking the non-finite verb 'hassen' in the complement clause.
Infinitive verb in the zu-infinitive complement of 'versuchen'; takes 'Mathe' as its object and is negated by 'nicht'.

translations.en: He's beginning to hate traffic jams.
translations.es: Está empezando a odiar los atascos.
translations.fr: Il commence à détester les embouteillages.
translations.it: Sta iniziando a odiare gli ingorghi.
translations.ko: 그는 차 막히는 걸 점점 싫어하게 되고 있어.
translations.pt: Ele está começando a odiar os engarrafamentos.
translations.vi: Anh ấy bắt đầu ghét kẹt xe.
translations.hi: वह ट्रैफिक जाम से नफ़रत करने लगा है.
translations.da: Han er begyndt at hade trafikkøer.
translations.ja: 彼は渋滞が嫌いになってきた。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun in nominative serving as subject of the finite verb.
Finite verb (3rd person singular, present) selecting a zu-infinitive complement.
Plural common noun functioning as the direct object within the infinitival clause ('zu hassen').
Infinitival particle marking the non-finite verb 'hassen' in the complement clause.
Infinitive verb in the zu-infinitive complement of 'beginnt'; takes 'Staus' as its object.

Wir versuchen, Mathe nicht zu hassen.

Sie scheint Kaffee plötzlich zu hassen.
Wir versuchen, Mathe nicht zu hassen.
en: She seems to suddenly hate coffee.
We're trying not to hate math.
He's beginning to hate traffic jams.
